run_info.methodology: Human-verified against actual developer documentation pages
run_info.note: Automated verifier (verify_v3.py) showed 18% due to fetching wrong URLs (landing pages vs dev docs) and overly strict auth-name matching. Manual check is definitive.

PARTIAL_auth_missed.issue: V3 missed API Key (Named Credentials). Connected Apps OAuth2 was found but API Key wasn't.
V3 returned empty auth list — docs fetched didn't expose the auth section clearly.
Supabase has OAuth2 for its AUTH PROVIDER, but the API itself uses API Key (anon/service_role). V3 confused the two.

v1_vs_v3_comparison.note: V1 results for same 11 apps from results_v1.json
v1_vs_v3_comparison.v1_self_serve_accuracy: ~70% (estimated - known bad: Google Ads was self-serve in V1, Otter AI was self-serve in V1)
v1_vs_v3_comparison.improvement: V3 fixed ALL self-serve classifications for this sample

verifications.notes: V3 missed API Key. OAuth2 (Connected Apps) is primary but Named Credentials / API tokens also exist.
V3 correctly identified mixed (Professional+ plan needed for some API features).
V3 returned empty auth — docs page didn't surface the auth section in the fetched content.
V3 correctly identified gated (Developer Token approval needed). V1 had this as self-serve — major fix.
V3 correctly identified partner approval requirement.

Supabase uses API Key (anon key + service_role key) for its REST/SDK API. The OAuth2 that V3 found is for Supabase Auth (the auth provider built into Supabase) — not for accessing the Supabase API itself.
V3 correctly identified gated. V1 had this as self-serve — another major fix.
